title: Crew Stability
query: SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CrewMemberQuits WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate) AS quits, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM KickCrewMember WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate) AS kicks

details.text: {quits} voluntary crew departure(s) and {kicks} kick(s) from hosted sessions during the report period. A captain with clean numbers runs enjoyable sessions — a high kick count might indicate strict discipline or repeated encounters with crew who weren't performing. A high quit count suggests something was driving people out.
details.help: CrewMemberQuits is voluntary departures; KickCrewMember is captain-initiated removals. quits drives the title as the turnover indicator.

tagLines: Crew session stability metrics compiled. Voluntary departures and removal actions noted.
Good crews stay put. The ones who left — some quit, some were asked to go. Both are recorded.
Crew stability is a captain's performance review. The numbers speak plainly.
